mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 70%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates above 4.5% indicate deteriorating labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed 1.5% in 60%+ of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and housing starts signal weakening supply-side activity, creating downward pressure on equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive when both INDPRO and HOUST decline by >1.5% sequentially.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth above 3% annualized indicates robust demand conditions that boost corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns fall below 0.5% in 50%+ of periods when GDPC1 exceeds 3% growth.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k jobs signals a tightening labor market that increases corporate hiring costs and reduces profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns exceed 1% in 60%+ of instances when PAYEMS exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% annualized triggers central bank hawkishness, increasing bond yields and reducing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed 1% in 50%+ of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% growth.

mechanism_narrative: A negative yield curve (10y-2y Treasury spread < 0) signals recession risk that reduces equity market optimism, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns exceed 1% in 60%+ of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL by >1.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising oil prices creates stagflationary pressures that depress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns exceed 2% in 50%+ of periods when INDPRO declines and CPIAUCSL rises by >0.5pp/month.
